full_prompt: Design a crew-accessible stowage rack for a deep-space habitat stowage benchmark, following a space-grant solicitation baseline. Rack outer envelope 1050 mm H x 530 mm W x 813 mm D, compliant with International Standard Payload Rack (ISPR) footprint; it must host four 406 mm x 330 mm x 254 mm cargo transfer bags (CTBs), each fully loaded to 27.2 kg per NASA-STD-3001. Rack hosts two pivoting crew-facing doors latched by paired Nomex-reinforced quarter-turn fasteners. Material: Aluminum 2024-T3 honeycomb panel (skin E = 73 GPa, face-sheet thickness 0.8 mm, core 25 mm Al honeycomb density 32 kg/m^3 with out-of-plane compression 1.5 MPa and shear 0.85 MPa, total panel areal density 5 kg/m^2) for walls + shelves; frame uses Al 6061-T6 extrusion. Environment: launched in SLS Block 1 cargo section under 9 g axial + 2 g lateral quasi-static + random vibration per NASA-HDBK-7005 spacecraft PSD, operated in-hab at 1 atm / 22 C nominal. Load cases: (LC1) launch quasi-static: 4 x 27.2 kg = 108.8 kg CTB mass inside rack + 35 kg rack mass, apply 9 g axial + 2 g lateral simultaneous; (LC2) random vib qual per NASA-HDBK-7005 (6.9 Grms from 20-2000 Hz, 2 min/axis); (LC3) crew operational load: 300 N vertical on an open door (crew stabilizing); (LC4) ECLSS hose routing reaction: 150 N lateral on the routing clip at top of rack; (LC5) thermal: rack surfaces 10-40 C nominal, during de-stow 5 C temporary exposure at exterior door; (LC6) hard-dock preload: frame bolts into habitat standoff at four corner posts with 5 kN preload each. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises in frame Al 6061-T6 under LC1 <= 184 MPa (yield 276 / 1.5); (R2) honeycomb panel face-sheet strain under LC1 <= 0.3%; (R3) core shear under LC1 <= 0.57 MPa (0.85 / 1.5); (R4) random-vib 3-sigma stress <= 184 MPa; (R5) first natural frequency of loaded rack above 35 Hz to stay above primary launch vehicle excitation band; (R6) rack dry mass <= 35 kg. Deliverables: STEP, quasi-static FEA, random-vib analysis, modal (10 modes), thermal FEA, mass report.
short_prompt:
Design a crew-accessible stowage rack for a deep-space habitat, hosting four 406 x 330 x 254 mm Cargo Transfer Bags at 27.2 kg each, with two pivoting crew-facing doors on quarter-turn fasteners. Walls and shelves are 2024-T3 Al honeycomb-skin sandwich panels over a 6061-T6 aluminum extrusion frame. Qualify per NASA-STD-3001 for CTB mass class and NASA-HDBK-7005 random-vib qualification; static cases at a 1.5 FoS on yield.

Envelope: International Standard Payload Rack (ISPR) footprint, 1050 mm H x 530 mm W x 813 mm D; rack dry mass target <= 35 kg.

Load cases.
- LC1 launch quasi-static: 9 g axial + 2 g lateral simultaneous on 4 x 27.2 kg CTB + rack.
- LC2 random vibration: NASA-HDBK-7005 profile, 6.9 Grms, 20-2000 Hz, 2 min per axis.
- LC3 crew door load: 300 N vertical on an open door.
- LC4 ECLSS hose reaction: 150 N lateral on the top routing clip.
- LC5 thermal: 5-40 C (in-hab 10-40 C nominal, 5 C transient at exterior door).
- LC6 dock preload: 5 kN axial at each of four corner posts into the habitat standoff.

prompt.title: Deep-space habitat stowage rack
prompt.brief: ISPR-footprint stowage rack for four 27.2 kg CTBs, surviving 9 g launch + random vib.
